text: What if species could evolve in a circle? Ring species are a fascinating example of evolution, connecting populations around the globe. Imagine a species that can breed with its neighbors, but not with those at the ends of the circle. One classic example is the greenish warbler. Two populations, separated by thousands of miles, show gradual changes. This challenges our ideas of species and evolution. It's not always black and white. Want to learn more about evolution? Hit that follow button!
